codeReview.summary: The feature correctly wires the Discover sidebar entry, switches the store to the persisted scan-root commands, preserves cached discovered results on mount, and adds broad component/store coverage. I did not find a blocking regression in the reviewed diff beyond the backend issues above, but two user-facing polish gaps remain in the shipped Discover UI.

issues.description: The reused InstallDialog still allows selecting multiple target platforms for a discovered skill, but handleInstallFromDialog loops over `agentIds` even though the first successful import removes the discovered row. If a user checks two platforms, the second iteration can only fail with `Discovered skill ... not found` after the first install succeeds.
issues.suggestedFix: Either constrain Discover installs to a single platform in the dialog or batch the backend import before deleting the discovered record.

issues.description: The Discover UI still contains hardcoded English copy (`N skill(s)` in the project/platform group headers and `No candidate directories found.` in DiscoverConfigDialog) even though the feature handoff says all discover strings are localized. In the default Chinese UI these literals bypass `t()` and stand out immediately.
issues.suggestedFix: Move the remaining literals into `discover.*` locale keys and format the group-count text through the translation layer.

summary: Pass with suggestions. The Discover frontend integration is in place and well-tested, but the platform-install dialog still exposes a multi-select path it cannot complete and a few Discover strings remain untranslated.
